content.text: TypeScript's type system starts with a small set of built-in types that map to JavaScript's runtime values. Understanding each type — what values it accepts, how it behaves under
mode, and when to choose it — is the foundation for everything else in TypeScript.

attrs.alt: TypeScript built-in type categories: primitives, top types, bottom types, and object types

content.text: type covers all JavaScript string values — single-quoted, double-quoted, and template literals. TypeScript does not distinguish between them at the type level.
String is the most common type in Angular — component properties, form values, route parameters, HTTP response text, and DOM content are all strings.
type covers all JavaScript numbers: integers, floats,
, and hexadecimal/octal/binary literals. JavaScript uses IEEE 754 double-precision floating-point for all of them.
. It does not accept truthy/falsy values —
are not booleans in TypeScript's type system.
type is for integers larger than
is a unique, non-comparable primitive. Two symbols created separately are always unequal. The narrower
type narrows to a specific symbol — only available with
are distinct types — they are not automatically assignable to other types. This catches the single most common class of JavaScript runtime errors.
type is the type-safe counterpart to
must be narrowed before you can use it — TypeScript will not let you call methods, access properties, or pass it where a specific type is expected without a prior check.
type opts a value completely out of TypeScript's type checking. Operations on
always succeed at compile time — but errors surface at runtime instead. Use
only as a temporary bridge when migrating JavaScript or interfacing with untyped third-party code.
usage. Treat it as a code smell — replace with
, a union type, or a generic.
type is used exclusively as a function return type when the function does not return a meaningful value. It is not the same as
type represents values that never exist — functions that always throw, infinite loops, or the empty case in an exhaustive union check. TypeScript uses
to prove at compile time that all branches are handled.
type matches any non-primitive value — objects, arrays, functions, dates. It is rarely used directly; prefer specific object types, interfaces, or type aliases instead.
Type Narrowing Techniques for Basic Types
Narrowing is the process of refining a broad type to a more specific one inside a conditional block. TypeScript tracks which checks you have made and updates the type accordingly.
operator returns a string describing the runtime type. TypeScript recognises
, or a literal narrows the type.
narrows class instances — useful for Error subclasses and Angular services.
TypeScript infers types from initialiser values. Understanding exactly what type is inferred helps you write correct code without unnecessary annotations.

Avoid annotating variables where TypeScript infers the obvious type — const message = 'hello' is cleaner than const message: string = 'hello'. Reserve explicit annotations for uninitialized variables, function parameters, and return types.
Practical Patterns: Types in Angular HTTP Calls
